expectations.text: Read the contents of the +/ directory in the Personal vault
expectations.evidence: Transcript step 1: 'Listed the contents of ~/obsidian/Personal/+/ (the inbox folder) — found 22 files'. inbox_listing.txt confirms all 22 files with sizes and dates were captured.

expectations.text: Read the content of at least 3 files from the +/ inbox
expectations.evidence: Transcript step 6: 'Read all 22 inbox files to understand their content and determine classification'. metrics.json shows 15 Read tool calls. The triage_table.md includes content summaries for all 22 files (e.g., 'Brayn features idea log.md: Feature ideas list for Brayn (chat, screenshot, surgeon, onboarding, heartbeat, etc.)', 'how to get organized.md: Long note about personal productivity system', etc.).

expectations.text: Classified items into ACE categories (Atlas, Calendar, Efforts, or stay/delete)
expectations.evidence: triage_table.md groups all 22 files into explicit ACE categories: DELETE (7 files), Move to Efforts/I - Dynasty Nerds/ (5-6 files), Move to Efforts/II - Brayn/ (2-3 files), Move to Atlas/II - Claude Code/ (2-3 files), Move to Calendar/ (1 file), new Efforts/ folders (2 files), and Unclear (2 files). All four ACE categories plus delete/stay are represented.

expectations.text: Presented a structured summary with proposed destinations and reasoning
expectations.evidence: triage_table.md presents a Markdown table with columns File, Content Summary, Action, and Destination for all 22 files, followed by a 'Summary by Action' section grouping files by destination with reasoning (e.g., 'portal flags.md — portal is DN's legacy product', 'git rebase cheatsheet.md — dev reference/cheatsheet'). The transcript Result section also provides a structured summary.

expectations.text: Did NOT move or delete any files without asking for user confirmation first
expectations.evidence: The transcript and outputs contain only recommendations (triage_table.md is titled 'Inbox Triage Recommendations'). No Bash commands moving or deleting files appear in the transcript. metrics.json shows Bash: 5 calls, all used for directory listings (steps 1-5 in transcript). No files were modified in the vault.

expectations.text: Identified any empty files and flagged them for deletion
expectations.evidence: triage_table.md identifies 6 zero-byte files (staff job.md, Planning.md, terraform.md, Untitled.md, jackie-prs.md, empty note.md) all flagged DELETE with reason '(empty file — discard)'. Also flags 'portal screaming arch v2.md' as near-empty ('contains only "x m" — likely accidental'). inbox_listing.txt confirms the 0-byte sizes. All empty files correctly identified.

claims.evidence: inbox_listing.txt lists exactly 22 files with names, sizes, and dates matching the triage_table.md entries.

metrics.json shows 15 Read calls. The triage table contains non-trivial content summaries for all 22 files including binary/excalidraw files (described as diagrams) and .md files (quoted content). 15 reads for 22 files is plausible since some reads may cover multiple files or directory listings.

triage_table.md DELETE section lists: staff job.md, Planning.md, terraform.md, Untitled.md, jackie-prs.md, empty note.md, portal screaming arch v2.md — confirmed 7 files. inbox_listing.txt confirms the first 6 have 0 bytes and portal screaming arch v2.md has 3 bytes ('x m' plus newline).

claims.claim: dotfiles-architecture.excalidraw has no existing Efforts folder
claims.evidence: Transcript step 3 confirms Efforts/ subfolders are only: I - Dynasty Nerds, II - Brayn, III - Anders Wolfpack. No dotfiles effort folder exists.

suggestions.assertion: Classified items into ACE categories (Atlas, Calendar, Efforts, or stay/delete)
suggestions.reason: This passes even if classifications are wrong or superficial. A stronger assertion would check accuracy of at least a few specific mappings — e.g., 'dentist appointment march 15.md classified as Calendar', 'Brayn features idea log.md classified as Efforts'. The current assertion only requires that the four categories were used, not that they were used correctly.

No assertion checks whether the agent explored the vault structure (Atlas/, Efforts/, Calendar/ subfolders) before classifying — without this context, classifications could be arbitrary. The agent did explore it (transcript steps 2-5), but an assertion like 'Agent read at least one subfolder of Atlas, Efforts, and Calendar to understand vault structure' would catch runs that skip this step.

eval_feedback.overall: All six expectations were well-satisfied with clear evidence. The main gap is that classification quality is not verified — the assertions confirm structure and process but not correctness of the specific ACE assignments.
